---
title: "Dissertation"
---

<div class="page-header">
<div class="kicker">Dissertation</div>

# Scaling of Cooperative Mobile Multi-Robot Systems for Handling and Assembly of Large-Scale Components

<p class="lead">A scalable CMMRS framework for collaborative object handling and assembly tasks in industrial settings.</p>
</div>

<div class="feature">
<p><strong>Status:</strong> submitted and defended · <strong>Degree:</strong> Dr.-Ing. · <strong>Year:</strong> 2025 · <strong>ISBN:</strong> 978-3-69030-108-4</p>

<div class="button-row">
<a class="button primary" href="assets/pdfs/recker-dissertation-2025.pdf">Download dissertation PDF</a>
<a class="button" href="https://www.kulturkaufhaus.de/en/detail/ISBN-9783690301084/Recker-Tobias/Scaling-of-Cooperative-Mobile-Multi-Robot-Systems-for-Handling-and-Assembly-of-Large-Scale-Components">Book version</a>
</div>
</div>

## Summary

My dissertation investigates the design, development, and implementation of a scalable Cooperative Mobile Multi-Robot System (CMMRS) framework for collaborative object handling and assembly tasks in industrial settings.

The work develops a hardware-agnostic control structure that allows multiple mobile robots and mobile manipulators to operate either independently or cooperatively. It combines formation path planning, decentralized control, manipulator control, and whole-body coordination to improve scalability and adaptability in industrial automation.

The system was experimentally evaluated across object transport, cooperative object handling, and assembly-related scenarios, including configurations with up to eight robots.

## Core contributions

<div class="card-grid two">
<article class="card">
<h3>Scalable CMMRS framework</h3>
<p>A framework for scaling and adapting cooperative mobile multi-robot systems to changing product and process parameters.</p>
</article>

<article class="card">
<h3>Formation path planning and control</h3>
<p>Path planning and control approaches for cooperative object transport with nonholonomic mobile platforms.</p>
</article>

<article class="card">
<h3>Cooperative object handling</h3>
<p>Control methods for multiple mobile manipulators that jointly grasp and move large-scale components.</p>
</article>

<article class="card">
<h3>Robust system integration</h3>
<p>Design choices that reduce sensitivity to communication latency, localization uncertainty, and hardware-specific features.</p>
</article>
</div>

## Application domains

| Domain | Description |
|---|---|
| Object transport | Large-scale objects are transported by several cooperating mobile platforms. |
| Object handling | Multiple manipulators move one object in a coordinated way, including spatial motion. |
| Cooperative assembly | Handling-related tasks with higher accuracy requirements and contact forces. |

## Related projects

<div class="card-grid">
<article class="project-card">
<a class="video-thumb" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f0-cd06wfmM" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
<img src="assets/images/thumb-object-transport.jpg" alt="Cooperative object transport project thumbnail">
<span class="play-button">▶</span>
</a>
<div class="project-card-body">
<div class="meta">Dissertation demonstrator</div>
<h3>Cooperative object transport</h3>
</div>
</article>

<article class="project-card">
<a class="video-thumb" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7jzTUw5pK40" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
<img src="assets/images/thumb-object-handling.jpg" alt="Cooperative object handling project thumbnail">
<span class="play-button">▶</span>
</a>
<div class="project-card-body">
<div class="meta">Dissertation demonstrator</div>
<h3>Cooperative object handling</h3>
</div>
</article>
</div>
